content.p: Write a statement involving the solutions of a vector equation that's equivalent to this claim: The set of vectors spans \mathbb R^4.

p.q: The vector equation {{veceqleft}}=\vec w has at least one solution for any \vec w\in\mathbb R^4.
For any \vec w\in\mathbb R^4, the following vector equation is consistent: {{veceqleft}}=\vec w.
At least one solution exists for the equation {{veceqleft}}=\vec w where \vec w is any \mathbb R^4 vector.
{{veceqleft}}=\vec w has at least one solution vector regardless of how \vec w is chosen from \mathbb R^4.
Given any \vec w from \mathbb R^4, a minimum of one solution for {{veceqleft}}=\vec w can be found.
We can find at least one vector which solves the equation {{veceqleft}}=\vec w no matter what \vec w\in\mathbb R^4 is.

content.p: Explain and demonstrate how to determine whether or not this statement is true.

outtro.p:
The vector equation (...), and thus the set of vectors
does span
does not span
\mathbb{R}^4.
